scope.claim_limit: Public urban KITTI seq05 check with more city traffic than seq00/07; not a substitute for dedicated high-dynamic multi-beam benchmarks.

id_lio.note: Indexed-point dynamic scan-to-map: pseudo-occupancy dynamic detection and delayed removal; no imu.csv so constant-velocity fallback. dynamic/frame=313.123506 corr/frame=2355.119522 removed_sum/frame=334147.129663

rf_lio_default.note: RF-LIO removal-first scan-to-map: adaptive multi-resolution range-image foreground removal before indexed dynamic scan-to-map; no imu.csv so constant-velocity fallback. removal_first/frame=210.783050 candidates/frame=210.783050 kept/frame=4289.216950 backend_dynamic/frame=274.204636 corr/frame=2184.345527 backend_removed_sum/frame=545292.499819

rf_lio_conservative.note: RF-LIO removal-first scan-to-map: adaptive multi-resolution range-image foreground removal before indexed dynamic scan-to-map; no imu.csv so constant-velocity fallback. removal_first/frame=158.940239 candidates/frame=158.940239 kept/frame=4341.059761 backend_dynamic/frame=280.836291 corr/frame=2203.469395 backend_removed_sum/frame=550353.636364

conclusion: On public KITTI Odometry seq05 full (urban driving), both dynamic paths stay active (ID-LIO dynamic/frame 313, RF-LIO removal_first/frame 211). Default RF-LIO RPE is 1.005% vs ID-LIO 0.702% (+43.2% relative) and KISS-ICP 0.617%. Conservative RF removal changes RPE by -1.2% vs default RF-LIO. This records mechanism behavior on a more dynamic public KITTI sequence but does not replace dedicated high-dynamic multi-beam benchmarks for manuscript-level dynamic-scene claims.
